title: Spicy Silky Croatian Prunes Stew
description: A pescatarian-friendly stew originating from Croatia, combining the sweetness of prunes with the heat of spices, all while cooking in a ttukbaegi-earthenware pot for a silky texture.
instructions: Heat olive oil in the ttukbaegi-earthenware pot over medium heat, then add chopped onions and cook until translucent.
Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add diced carrots, chopped prunes, diced potatoes, spicy paprika, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Pour in enough water to cover the ingredients and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and let simmer for 30 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the pot from the heat and let it cool slightly before serving.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley, if desired.
Serve hot, garnished with a sprinkle of paprika.
This stew is best served with crusty bread or over mashed potatoes.
For a thicker stew, mash some of the potatoes and return them to the pot.
For a thinner stew, add a bit more water.
This stew freezes well, so feel free to make a large batch and freeze for later.
